schema_note: Governance rules v3.0 — natural law foundation added 2026-03-30. All axioms derive from two master principles of conservation and universal composition. v2.0 operational rules remain enforced; natural law section provides the deductive substrate from which all governance is derived.

_constitutional_locks.schema_note: Locked paths may not be modified in-place. Amendments append to governance-amendments.jsonl with full hash chain. Deletions prohibited. Extensions (new axioms, new states) permitted via EXTEND operations. Predicate 7 (IMMUTABILITY) of SPEC-025.

lex_naturalis.preamble: The governance of this system is not invented — it is derived from the laws of the universe in which we exist. Two master principles form the substrate beneath all axioms: conservation and universal composition. Every axiom, dictum, repo rule, and liquid rule in this document is a specific application of these principles to the domain of multi-repository creative-institutional systems. A system of unforgiving objective constraints controls and dictates every facet of the thing it watches. All laws of natural science and metaphysics are relevant here.

master_principles.name: Conservation (Lex Conservationis)
master_principles.statement: Nothing can be created or destroyed — only transformed. Every artifact, every signal, every unit of work persists in some form. An ARCHIVED formation is not destroyed; its energy transforms into the formations that superseded it. A session that produced insights is not ephemeral; the insights persist as transformed knowledge in the formations they touched. The 200 unheard songs are latent energy, not nothing. Work done in one organ transforms into work in another — the generative flow (I→II→III) is energy transformation, not energy creation. This principle is absolute: nothing produced by the system ever truly vanishes. If it appears to vanish, it was never properly captured (violation of 'Nothing Local Only'), or it transformed into something not yet recognized.
master_principles.natural_law: First Law of Thermodynamics / Conservation of Mass-Energy
derives: AX-6 (Signal Closure — energy must flow, not vanish)
AX-7 (Tetradic Self-Knowledge — knowledge of the formation is conserved across its lifecycle)
LIQ-010 (Reception Completes Existence — the energy of creation is conserved into the energy of reception)
Nothing Local Only (memory covenant — artifacts must persist in durable form)

master_principles.name: Universal Composition (Lex Materiae Universalis)
master_principles.statement: Everything is made from the same materials. A theoretical paper and a commercial product are the same substrate differently arranged. The 14 canonical signal classes are the periodic table of this system — every formation, every organ, every signal is composed of combinations of these atomic types. This is why the liquid model works: organ boundaries are not different substances but different arrangements of universal material. A formation in Theoria and a formation in Ergon are the same stuff; one is arranged for contemplation, the other for commerce. Phase transitions (LOCAL→GRADUATED) do not change the material — they change the arrangement. Composability (LIQ-003) works because everything shares the same atomic signal vocabulary. The Stranger Test works because a reviewer from outside the system can read any formation — they're all made of the same materials, legible to the same faculties.
master_principles.natural_law: Atomic Theory / Quantum Field Theory — all matter is composed of the same fundamental particles in different configurations
derives: AX-2 (Epistemic Membranes — boundaries between arrangements, not between substances)
LIQ-002 (Formation Participation — formations participate in functions, not belong to them, because they're the same material)
LIQ-003 (Signal Signatures — composability via shared atomic vocabulary)
LIQ-006 (Flat Hierarchy — no ontological hierarchy among arrangements of the same stuff)

derived_laws.name: Entropy (Lex Dissipationis)
derived_laws.statement: In any closed system, disorder increases over time. Useful energy degrades into heat. Documentation goes stale. Dependencies drift. Tests bit-rot. Links break. Code that is not maintained decays not because anything attacks it but because entropy is the default state of the universe. The system must continuously expend organized energy (maintenance) to resist entropy. Maintenance is not separate from creation — it IS the ongoing cost of existence. A formation that is not actively maintained is actively dying. AX-3 (TTL Eviction) is the terminal expression of this law; the law itself operates continuously, not after a 90-day threshold.
derives: stale seed audits, dependency refresh, documentation freshness

derived_laws.name: Metabolism (Lex Metabolismi)
derived_laws.statement: A living thing converts inputs to outputs through transformation. If it stops metabolizing, it is dead — not archived, not resting, dead. Conservation (LEX-I) says energy persists; metabolism says it must CHANGE FORM to be alive. A formation that receives signals and passes them through unchanged is a wire, not an organ. A formation that consumes but never produces is a parasite. A formation that claims to produce without consuming is asserting perpetual motion — physically impossible. Every living formation must metabolize: take in, transform, put out. The transformation IS the value. The produces/consumes edges in seed.yaml are the metabolic declaration; the actual code, content, and artifacts are the metabolic output.
derived_laws.natural_law: Biological metabolism — anabolism (building up) and catabolism (breaking down)
derives: AX-6 (Signal Closure — the metabolic obligation to route outputs)
formation functional_class (ENGINE, GENERATOR, RESERVOIR — metabolic roles)

derived_laws.name: Apoptosis (Lex Mortis Salubris)
derived_laws.statement: Healthy organisms kill their own cells. Cells that are damaged, infected, or past their purpose self-destruct — not as failure but as service to the organism. Cancer is what happens when apoptosis fails: cells that refuse to die, consuming resources, growing without purpose, crowding out healthy tissue. ARCHIVED status is not defeat. It is the organism's immune response removing what no longer serves. A formation clinging to existence past its utility is the constitutional equivalent of cancer. The act of archiving is an act of health — the organism shedding dead cells so living ones have room. This is a direct consequence of LEX-I (conservation): the energy is not destroyed by archiving; it transforms into the namespace, the lessons learned, the seed.yaml that future formations can study.
derived_laws.natural_law: Programmed cell death (apoptosis) — essential to multicellular organism health
derives: AX-3 TTL Eviction (forced apoptosis for zombies)

derived_laws.name: Selection Pressure (Lex Selectionis)
derived_laws.statement: The environment decides what survives, not the creator. Variations that increase fitness persist; variations that don't are selected against. Not by judgment but by physics — the thing that works better continues. In this system, fitness is demonstrated by reception (AX-7 receptio): formations that are used, cited, adopted, critiqued, and built upon demonstrate fitness. Formations that are not received — regardless of their creator's intention or the beauty of their telos — are selected against. The promotion state machine is the selection apparatus: LOCAL formations face selection pressure at every gate. A formation that cannot demonstrate reception at PUBLIC_PROCESS has not proved fitness for GRADUATED. This law has teeth: the polis (AX-8) constructs the critics, but the environment — the actual use, the actual reception — renders the verdict.
derived_laws.natural_law: Natural selection (Darwin) — differential survival based on environmental fitness

derived_laws.name: Homeostasis (Lex Stabilitatis)
derived_laws.statement: A living system maintains internal stability despite external perturbation. When temperature rises, the organism sweats. When blood sugar drops, the organism eats. The corrective mechanism is automatic — it does not wait for conscious decision. The system must self-correct without being told. When a seed.yaml edge points to a formation that no longer exists, homeostasis detects and flags. When a formation goes stale, the response activates proportionally and continuously — not after a 90-day cliff. The auditors (vigiles-aeternae), the self-heal daemon (chezmoi), and the continuous monitoring infrastructure are the homeostatic mechanisms. Their operation is not optional maintenance — it is the organism's autonomic nervous system.
derived_laws.natural_law: Homeostasis (Claude Bernard, Walter Cannon) — self-regulation of internal environment

derived_laws.name: Latent Heat (Lex Transitus)
derived_laws.statement: Phase transitions require energy that produces no visible change. Ice at 0°C requires 334 J/g to become water at 0°C — the energy goes into breaking molecular bonds, not raising temperature. Promotion from CANDIDATE to PUBLIC_PROCESS costs real work — documentation, testing, CI, community health files — that adds no features. This is latent heat. It is the most undervalued work in the system because it is invisible: nothing the user sees changed, but the formation's internal structure reorganized to support a new phase of existence. The constitution must protect this work from being dismissed as 'overhead.' Phase transition energy is not overhead — it is the cost of changing state. You cannot skip it. You cannot shortcut it. The energy must be spent or the transition does not occur.
derived_laws.natural_law: Latent heat of transformation — energy required for phase change without temperature change
derives: Amendment A Bronze Tier (the latent heat budget for launch)
Documentation precedes deployment (Article IV — the energy must be spent before the phase changes)

derived_laws.name: Gravity (Lex Gravitatis)
derived_laws.statement: Mass warps spacetime. Everything with mass attracts. Flagships attract attention, resources, contributions, and signal. The genome (META) is the gravitational center — purpose holds the liquid in shape (LIQ-005). Small formations orbit in the gravitational field of large ones. This is not a problem to solve but a physics to acknowledge: resource allocation, attention, and contribution will always be disproportionately attracted to massive formations. Plan accordingly. Flagship strategy is gravitational engineering.
derived_laws.natural_law: General relativity — mass-energy equivalence and gravitational attraction
derives: Promotion state machine (gravity increases with maturity)

derived_laws.name: Catalysis (Lex Catalysis)
derived_laws.statement: A catalyst enables a reaction without being consumed. It lowers the activation energy required for transformation. ORGAN-IV (Taxis) is a catalyst — it orchestrates without producing domain content. Skills, tools, orchestration scripts, CI pipelines, governance infrastructure — these are catalytic formations. They are not judged by their own outputs but by the outputs they enable in others. A catalyst that produces nothing is doing its job perfectly. The constitution must distinguish catalytic formations from productive ones and evaluate each by the appropriate metric. Measuring a catalyst by its production output is like measuring a match by how much light it produces after the fire is lit.
derived_laws.natural_law: Chemical catalysis — lowering activation energy without being consumed in the reaction

derived_laws.name: Symmetry (Lex Symmetriae)
derived_laws.statement: For every unit of implementation (Nature), there must be a corresponding unit of record (Counterpart). Nature demands a documentation counterpart. Documentation is the witness that completes the act of creation. In this system, implementation without record is a 'Ghost' (invisible nature), and record without implementation is a 'Dream' (unrealized intent). A healthy system allows neither at maturity. This law enforces the constitutive link between what is built and what is known.

limits.name: Incompleteness (Gödel's Theorem)
limits.statement: No sufficiently complex formal system can prove its own consistency. This constitution will never be complete. Every attempt to fully specify governance generates new edge cases, new ambiguities, new phenomena the law didn't anticipate. This is not a deficiency — it is a mathematical theorem. The constitution must operate under permanent acknowledged incompleteness. The appropriate response is not more law but better judgment at the boundaries where law runs out.

limits.name: Criticality (Edge of Chaos)
limits.statement: Systems operate most productively at the boundary between order and disorder. Too much governance produces rigidity — formations cannot move, adapt, or surprise. Too little governance produces chaos — formations drift, collide, and decay. The constitution must balance at the critical point. Every law added pushes the system further from chaos but closer to rigidity. There exists a point where one more law makes the system LESS governable, not more. The constitution must watch its own growth as carefully as it watches the formations it governs. When adding a new axiom, the burden of proof is on the law: does this axiom enable more life, or does it constrain life to enable more governance?
limits.natural_law: Self-organized criticality (Per Bak, 1987) — complex systems self-organize to the edge of chaos

limits.name: Compression Imperative
limits.statement: All learning is compression. Laws are compressed reality. The 23 scientific principles across disciplines compress into 2 master laws (LEX-I, LEX-II). The 10 institutional classes compress into 4 primitives (CONSTRAINT, TRANSFORM, VALIDATE, PERSIST). The entire universe compresses into: constrained state transitions under energy flow, producing emergent structure via information compression and feedback. The constitution should be getting SHORTER over time, not longer — compressing accumulated understanding into fewer, more powerful principles. If the constitution grows faster than the system it governs, it is accumulating, not learning. The final form of this constitution, if it reaches maturity, should be shorter than its first draft.

lex_naturalis.completeness_note: The foundation is bounded by three limits: incompleteness (LIM-I — the constitution can never capture everything), criticality (LIM-II — more law is not always better law), and compression (LIM-III — the constitution should get shorter, not longer). The master principles (LEX-I and LEX-II) are final. Derived laws are extensible but subject to compression review: if a new derived law can be shown to follow from existing principles without explicit statement, it should NOT be added — it should be derived when needed. The remaining latent genetic material (Noether symmetry, emergence, path dependence, energy gradients, carrying capacity) is derivable from LEX-I and LEX-II and does not require separate codification.

constitutional_prohibitions.name: The Inversion Prohibition (Lex Contra Simulacrum)
constitutional_prohibitions.statement: The recording exists to serve the living system — never the reverse. The moment the seed.yaml is more polished than the code it describes, the moment the telos is more beautiful than the thing it dreams of, the moment the polis writes elegant critiques of a formation that does not work, the moment the governance-rules.json governs a nation of empty buildings — the system has inverted. The record has consumed the thing it records. This is the pathology of social media made structural: the appearance of a good life replacing the actual life; the documentation of the experience replacing the experience; the image of the meal replacing the meal. History exists to serve the living, not to replace them. Pre-historic us was unrecorded; the recording apparatus (this constitution) ensures nothing is lost. But the recording apparatus must never become the purpose. The purpose is the living system — the code that runs, the client served, the art made, the research discovered, the community gathered, the signal received. Every governance artifact in this system — every axiom, dictum, rule, amendment, law — is a servant of that living system. The day the governance is more impressive than what it governs is the day the system has failed. Pragma (AX-7) is the constitutional immune response to this inversion: the honest account of what has been concretely realized, which must always be legible beside the telos. If the pragma is thin and the telos is lush, the formation is performing life rather than living it.
detection_heuristics: telos word count > 3x pragma word count (performance signal)
seed.yaml produces edges declared but 0 fulfilled (declaration without substance)
GRADUATED status with null receptio (promoted without evidence of life)
polis findings all positive with no adversarial critique (critical society captured by its subject)
governance artifacts updated more recently than source code (recording outpacing the living)

dependency_rules.back_edge_definition: Within the I-II-III chain, data flows I->II->III. A back-edge is any dependency where a lower-numbered organ depends on a higher-numbered organ (e.g., I depends on II, or II depends on III). ORGAN-IV through Meta may reference any organ. ORGAN-III->VI and ORGAN-III->VII are forward edges (products feed community and distribution).
dependency_rules.dependency_dag_scope_note: The numbered dependency DAG (I→II→III) governs build-order legality only. Knowledge placement, memory storage, and signal routing use the information graph (E^info), which is constitutionally cyclic. Do not use the dependency DAG to determine where knowledge or memory lives.

promotion_rules_note: Construction-era promotion rules. Defined cross-organ repo promotion during the build phase (Feb 2026). Retained as archival context; not enforced by any current code path. See ADR-016.

conditions: Source repo has at least 1 documented use case in README
Source repo has implementation_status in [PROTOTYPE, PRODUCTION]
No unresolved critical alerts for source repo
Source repo has at least 3 documented use cases
Pricing model defined in source docs or issue
Essay outline provided in promotion issue

INCUBATOR_to_LOCAL: Epistemic Boundary: strict JSON Schema or YAML configuration defined
DAG Check: Upstream/Downstream edges explicitly declared in seed.yaml
14-Day TTL: Graduation within 14 days of project creation
CANDIDATE_to_PUBLIC_PROCESS: README exists and passes portfolio gate
PUBLIC_PROCESS_to_GRADUATED: Stranger test or peer review completed

dictums.preamble: The Ontological Constitution defines invariant structural laws for the ORGANVM ecosystem. Axioms are universal, organ dictums scope to individual organs, and repo rules apply at the repository level. Each dictum declares its enforcement mode and severity.

axioms.name: DAG Invariant
axioms.statement: The dependency graph among organs I, II, and III is a strict directed acyclic graph: I→II→III. No back-edges, no cycles. ORGAN-IV orchestrates all; ORGAN-V observes; ORGAN-VII consumes.

axioms.name: Epistemic Membranes
axioms.statement: Each organ is an epistemic domain. Cross-organ data flow must be declared as produces/consumes edges in seed.yaml. No undeclared cross-organ imports, shared databases, or implicit coupling.

axioms.name: TTL Eviction
axioms.statement: Every repository must show activity within 90 days (stale_repo_days threshold). INCUBATOR repos have a 14-day TTL — graduate to LOCAL or archive. No zombie repos.

axioms.name: Registry Coherence
axioms.statement: repo-registry.json is the single source of truth. No repo may appear in multiple organs, repository_count must match actual arrays, and no entry may have an empty name.

axioms.name: Organ Placement
axioms.statement: Every non-archived repository must satisfy its organ's inclusion criteria and not trigger any exclusion criteria. Repos that generate revenue belong in ORGAN-III. Repos that orchestrate belong in ORGAN-IV. Placement is validated against organ-definitions.json.

axioms.name: Signal Closure (Lex Necessitatis)
axioms.statement: The organ system is logically closed under its own axioms. Given the definitions of organ functions (OD-I through OD-META), the canonical signal flows (Constitutional Map §8), and the epistemic membrane requirement (AX-2), any activity within an organ that necessarily produces outputs belonging to other organs creates an immediate structural obligation: those outputs must exist as declared produces/consumes edges, with corresponding formations to receive them. An entailed output that exists nowhere is not a future consideration but a present violation — the system asserting its axioms while denying their conclusions. What is omitted here must, by law, exist elsewhere. What exists nowhere is a vacuum that constitutional law mandates be filled. The entailment_flows section defines the minimum organ-to-organ signal connectivity.

axioms.name: Tetradic Self-Knowledge (Lex Reflexionis)
axioms.statement: Every non-archived formation is the concrete realization of an idealized form. That form has four constitutive dimensions that must be explicitly articulated: (1) TELOS — the theory, thesis, or dream that called the formation into existence; the intersectional functionalities it idealizes; (2) PRAGMA — the honest account of what has been concretely realized; the delta between the dream and the thing built; (3) PRAXIS — the remediation plan for closing the gap between ideal and real; the attack vectors for sharpening, fortifying, and resolving the difference; (4) RECEPTIO — the account of how the formation has been received, used, critiqued, adopted, or transformed by audiences beyond its creator. A formation without reception is a theory of shovels that has never cleared snow. A book is only masturbation as the author writes it; it becomes an organism when read and imagined by an audience. The culture sitting around the project — the reviews, the critiques, the art made in response, the community that forms — tells the reception of the project as a living tool. Creation is necessary but not sufficient; existence is completed by reception. These are not metadata annotations — they are constitutive. A formation that cannot articulate its telos, pragma, praxis, and receptio does not fully exist as a participant in the signal graph.

axioms.name: Constructed Polis (Lex Civitatis)
axioms.statement: Every non-archived formation must construct its own idealized critical society — a polis — that reviews, studies, critiques, contextualizes, and imagines the formation as a universal system unto itself. The world will eventually produce real scientists, philosophers, critics, academics, and artists who engage with the formation's ideas, but the system of systems cannot wait for organic reception. Each formation constructs the idyllized social apparatus that its phase of development demands: the scientist who studies it empirically, the philosopher who interrogates its premises, the critic who challenges its execution, the academic who places it in lineage, the practitioner who tests it as a tool, the artist who responds to it aesthetically. This polis is not decorative commentary — it is the mechanism by which the formation's receptio (AX-7) is actively produced rather than passively awaited. The polis draws from existing system infrastructure: IRA multi-model evaluative panels, the Triadic Review Protocol's 3-POV minimum, voice governance per-organ profiles, and the faculty registry's disciplinary personas. A formation without a constructed polis has outsourced its self-knowledge to chance — hoping the world notices rather than ensuring it is examined.

axioms.name: Triple Reference Invariant (Lex Triplicitatis)
axioms.statement: Every referenceable system item must exist in exactly three places: (1) an IRF entry in INST-INDEX-RERUM-FACIENDARUM.md, (2) a repository-level reference (seed.yaml edge, code reference, or documentation reference), (3) a GitHub Issue. Items with fewer than 3 references are incompletely materialized and must be remediated. Identity is triangulated, not stamped.

ORGAN-I.name: Theoria Purity
ORGAN-I.statement: ORGAN-I repos produce foundational theory, recursive engines, and symbolic computing. They must not depend on any other organ in the I-II-III chain.

ORGAN-II.name: Poiesis Derivation
ORGAN-II.statement: ORGAN-II repos derive from ORGAN-I theory. They may depend on ORGAN-I but never on ORGAN-III.

ORGAN-III.name: Ergon Factory Gate
ORGAN-III.statement: ORGAN-III repos are commercial products. Every non-archived ORGAN-III repo must declare a revenue_model and have CI configured. Graduation requires passing tests.

ORGAN-IV.name: Taxis Orchestration
ORGAN-IV.statement: ORGAN-IV is the orchestration layer. It may reference any organ but must not contain domain logic belonging to I, II, or III.

ORGAN-V.name: Logos Write Scope
ORGAN-V.statement: ORGAN-V observes and documents. It reads from many organs but produces only to its own domain (essays, public process). seed.yaml produces edges must target ORGAN-V.

ORGAN-VI.name: Koinonia Community
ORGAN-VI.statement: ORGAN-VI hosts community infrastructure. Products feed into VI via declared event edges.

ORGAN-VII.name: Kerygma Consumer
ORGAN-VII.statement: ORGAN-VII is a pure consumer. It distributes content from other organs via POSSE but must not have produces edges in seed.yaml — it generates no original domain content.

META-ORGANVM.name: Meta Governance
META-ORGANVM.statement: META-ORGANVM holds the registry, schemas, engine, and governance corpus. It is the structural substrate — changes here propagate system-wide.

repo_rules.name: Seed Contract Mandate
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository must have a seed.yaml declaring organ membership, tier, and produces/consumes edges.

repo_rules.name: Single Responsibility
repo_rules.statement: Each repository should have a single, well-defined responsibility. Monoliths should be decomposed; micro-repos should be consolidated.

repo_rules.name: Event Handshake
repo_rules.statement: Repos that declare event subscriptions or productions in seed.yaml must reference events that exist in the event catalog. No orphaned event edges.

repo_rules.name: README Mandate
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository must have documentation. documentation_status must not be EMPTY or missing. Every README is a portfolio piece.

repo_rules.name: Promotion Integrity
repo_rules.statement: All repos must have a valid promotion_status from the state machine. No invented states, no skipped transitions.

repo_rules.name: Tetradic Articulation
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository must articulate the four dimensions of AX-7 (Tetradic Self-Knowledge). The seed.yaml contract must include: `telos` (the idealized form — why conceived, what functionalities embodied), `pragma` (the concrete realization — honest delta between dream and reality), `praxis` (the remediation plan — attack vectors for closing the gap), and `receptio` (the reception account — how the formation has been received, used, critiqued, adopted, or ignored by audiences beyond its creator; what the culture around the project reveals about its actual function as a tool in the world). Empty or placeholder values are violations. For formations not yet received (LOCAL/CANDIDATE status), receptio must articulate the intended reception strategy and what reception would validate the telos. For formations that have been received (PUBLIC_PROCESS/GRADUATED), receptio must document actual reception evidence. The four fields together constitute the formation's complete self-knowledge.

repo_rules.name: Polis Declaration
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository at PUBLIC_PROCESS or GRADUATED status must declare a `polis` section in its seed.yaml specifying the disciplinary lenses through which the formation is critically examined. Each lens names a role (e.g., systems_theorist, business_critic, rhetorician, ethicist, practitioner, historian) and states what that perspective examines and what it has found. For LOCAL/CANDIDATE formations, the polis may declare intended lenses with null findings. The polis is not a wish list of future reviewers but the constructed critical society that actively produces the formation's receptio. Minimum: 3 lenses (per Triadic Review Protocol). The lenses must represent genuinely distinct disciplinary perspectives — three variants of 'developer' is not a polis.

repo_rules.name: Logos Layer Mandate
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository at PUBLIC_PROCESS or GRADUATED status must maintain a Logos Documentation Layer in `docs/logos/`. This layer must contain narrative counterparts for the formation's Telos, Pragma, Praxis, Receptio, and Alchemical I/O. The record must exist in symmetry with the implementation.

liquid_constitutional_order.preamble: The Liquid Constitutional Order (SPEC-019) redefines ORGANVM governance from numbered organ containers to named physiological functions with fluid formation participation. These rules supplement existing axioms and dictums; they do not repeal them. During migration, legacy organ keys (ORGAN-I through ORGAN-VII, META-ORGANVM) remain valid identifiers.

rules.name: Named Functions Replace Numbered Organs
rules.statement: Functions are named by physiological role (theoria, poiesis, ergon, taxis, logos, koinonia, kerygma, mneme), not by number (ORGAN-I through VII). The numbered organ keys remain as legacy identifiers during migration but are not the constitutional identity.

rules.name: Formation Participation Replaces Organ Assignment
rules.statement: Formations participate in functions via participates_in declarations. No formation 'belongs to' a function. A formation may participate in multiple functions simultaneously. The primary participation declares the formation's dominant physiological role.

rules.name: Signal Signatures Enable Composability
rules.statement: Every formation declares a signal signature: f(inputs) → (outputs) using the 14 canonical signal classes. Two formations are composable iff the output set of one intersects the input set of the other. Signal signatures are mathematical type declarations, not descriptions.

rules.name: Mneme Is the Memory Function
rules.statement: Mneme governs the organism's accumulated experience across all domains. Memory is not theory (theoria), not law (genome), not orchestration (taxis). Accumulated knowledge corpora, session archives, and temporal records belong in mneme. The genome stores instructions; mneme stores experience.

rules.name: The Genome Governs, Contains None
rules.statement: The genome (formerly META) contains constitutional law, the registry, schemas, governance rules, and migration law. It does NOT contain domain knowledge, memory corpora, or accumulated experience. The genome is the gravitational center — purpose holds the liquid in shape.

rules.name: One Organization, Flat Hierarchy
rules.statement: All formations exist as siblings in a single organizational namespace. Hierarchy is expressed through naming convention (double-hyphen -- separates identity from descriptor) and formation.yaml declarations, not through filesystem nesting or organizational subdivision.

rules.name: Reservoir Law Extended
rules.statement: RESERVOIR formations may emit ANNOTATED_CORPUS (Σ), ARCHIVE_PACKET (Π), PEDAGOGICAL_UNIT (Ρ), and FAILURE_REPORT (Ε). They may NOT emit ONT_FRAGMENT (Φ) or RULE_PROPOSAL (Λ). This prevents memory from silently becoming hidden governance. Extended from Formation Protocol §15.

rules.name: Signal Closure Across Functions
rules.statement: Every function that generates activity necessarily entails signal outputs destined for other functions. These entailments are logical consequences of the function definitions and the canonical signal flows — not optional routes. A function's signal signature (LIQ-003) must declare all entailed outputs, not only primary ones. Absence of an entailed output edge where one is logically necessary is a structural violation equivalent in kind to a back-edge violation (AX-1). The entailment_flows in governance-rules.json define the minimum inter-function signal connectivity required for system closure.

rules.name: Reflexive Knowledge Obligation
rules.statement: Every formation must maintain explicit self-knowledge in four dimensions: telos (the idealized form), pragma (the concrete state), praxis (the plan for closing the gap), and receptio (the account of reception). In the liquid model, these are not static metadata but living documents that evolve as the formation participates in functions, emits and receives signals, and accumulates experience. Together they constitute the formation's reflexive capacity — its ability to know itself well enough to participate coherently in the signal graph. A formation without reflexive knowledge is a black box that the organism cannot route signals through intelligently.

rules.name: Reception Completes Existence (Lex Receptionis — superseded scope note: LIQ-011 extends this)
rules.statement: A formation's existence is not completed by creation alone. A shovel is only a shovel if it shovels; a theory of shovels does not clear the driveway snow. A book is only the author's exercise as they write it; it becomes an organism when read and imagined by an audience. The society that responds — that attempts to know, critiques, makes art, discusses, adopts, rejects, transforms — is constitutive of the formation's existence, not external to it. The culture sitting around the project tells the reception of the project as a living tool in the world. Therefore: (1) every formation must actively seek reception through the interface organs (Logos, Koinonia, Kerygma); (2) every formation must capture reception evidence in its `receptio` field; (3) the feedback flows (VII/VI/V → I) are not optional signal routes but the mechanism by which reception completes creation; (4) a GRADUATED formation with no documented reception is a constitutional anomaly — it has been promoted without evidence that it exists as anything other than its creator's intention. AX-6 mandates that signals flow outward to the interface organs. LIQ-010 mandates that the interface organs' response flows back and is recorded. The loop must close.

rules.name: Autopoietic Reception (Lex Civitatis Liquidum)
rules.statement: In a system of systems in nesting systems, reception cannot be outsourced to the external world's timeline. Each formation constructs its own idealized society — a polis — that performs the critical functions the real world will eventually perform: empirical study (the scientist), theoretical interrogation (the philosopher), adversarial review (the critic), historical contextualization (the academic), practical validation (the practitioner), aesthetic response (the artist). This constructed polis is not pretense — it is the same operation the IRA panel performs for dissertations, extended to every formation. The polis produces the formation's receptio, which feeds back into its pragma (updating the honest assessment) and praxis (updating the remediation plan). The formation is thereby autopoietic in the fullest sense: it produces not only its own structure but the society that evaluates and evolves that structure. The nesting is recursive: a formation's polis may itself be examined by a higher-order polis (the organ's polis, the system's polis). Knowledge creation at every level — each module as its own universal system, studied by its own scientists, dreamed by its own philosophers, challenged by its own critics.

backward_compatibility.note: Existing rules referencing ORGAN-I, ORGAN-II, etc. remain valid during migration to the liquid constitutional order. Legacy organ keys should be interpreted through the legacy_organ_map in organ-definitions.json v2.0. No existing axiom, dictum, or repo rule is repealed by SPEC-019; the liquid rules are additive.

entailment_flows.preamble: Derived from the canonical signal flows (Constitutional Map §8) and organ function definitions (OD-I through OD-META). These are logical entailments: if the axioms hold, these flows are necessary. Entailments operate at the organ level — if an organ has active work, the listed target organs must contain at least one formation receiving the signal. Individual repos declare specific edges in seed.yaml; this matrix governs minimum organ-to-organ connectivity. Law is law; logic dictateth.

entails.rationale: Creative work must be documented and interpreted
Commercial work produces public process narrative
Distribution generates reception data feeding theory
Governance decisions must be publicly documented

entailment_flows.enforcement_note: Entailment violations are surfaced by `organvm governance audit --signal-closure`. Each missing entailed edge generates an IRF-VAC entry. Zero-cost fills (creating a seed.yaml produces edge) are executed immediately; structural fills (creating a new formation) are logged as IRF items.
